meta.title: Web Design & Development
meta.description: Professional web design and development services using open-source technologies. Modern, responsive websites built for sustainability and performance.

hero.title: Web Design & Development
hero.subtitle: 100% commitment to freedom through open source, decentralization, privacy, data ownership, code ownership, and maximum automation. We create digital experiences where effort becomes a choice, not a necessity.

values.title: Our Core Values
values.subtitle: Every website we build serves one ultimate goal: your freedom. Every principle works together to transform effort from necessity into choice.

openSource.title: Open Source
openSource.description: Transparent, auditable code that you can fully inspect, modify, and own. No proprietary black boxes or vendor lock-ins.

decentralization.title: Decentralization
decentralization.description: Break free from Big Tech platforms. Host your website anywhere, preserve your independence, and avoid single points of failure.

privacyFirst.title: Privacy First
privacyFirst.description: Minimal tracking, secure authentication, and transparent data handling. Your users' privacy is protected by design, not as an afterthought.

ownYourData.title: Own Your Data
ownYourData.description: Your data belongs to you, not the tech giants. Full control over where it is stored, how it is used, and who accesses it.

ownYourCode.title: Own Your Code
ownYourCode.description: Complete ownership of your website's source code. Modify, extend, or migrate at any time without restrictions or licensing fees.

maxAutomation.title: Maximum Automation
maxAutomation.description: Seamless automated workflows that minimize manual effort while upholding all our core principles. 100% automation is our goal.

userExperience.title: User Experience
userExperience.description: Intuitive interfaces that demand minimal effort from users while delivering maximum functionality and productivity.

developerExperience.title: Developer Experience
developerExperience.description: Clean, maintainable code with excellent tooling and documentation, making development and maintenance a pleasure.

philosophy.title: Our Freedom-First Philosophy
philosophy.intro: We believe in 100% commitment to freedom through maximum automation.
philosophy.automation: When automation minimizes unwanted effort, it transforms effort from necessity into choice — and choice is freedom.

freedomPrinciple.label: The Freedom Principle
freedomPrinciple.text: 100% commitment to freedom means every aspect of your digital presence should serve your autonomy, not restrict it.

automationLiberation.label: Automation as Liberation
automationLiberation.text: Maximum automation eliminates repetitive, unwanted tasks, giving you the freedom to focus on what truly matters to you and your mission.

choiceNotCoercion.label: Choice Over Coercion
choiceNotCoercion.text: When systems work seamlessly without your constant intervention, every moment you invest becomes intentional — a choice, not a necessity.

philosophy.quote: True freedom is not just the ability to choose — it's systems that preserve your right to choose by eliminating forced effort.
philosophy.ratingIntro: Every website and web app we build receives a comprehensive freedom rating based on how well it supports your autonomy:

commitment.label: Our Commitment
commitment.text: We aim for maximum scores across all freedom dimensions. Every trade-off is discussed transparently, with alternatives always explored.

servicesSection.title: Our Web Development Services
servicesSection.subtitle: Comprehensive web solutions built with open-source technologies and sustainable practices.
servicesSection.consultationText: We begin every project with a thorough consultation to understand your needs, goals, and technical requirements. This helps us deliver accurate estimates and ensure we are the right fit for your project.

customDev.title: Custom Web Development
customDev.description: Tailor-made web applications built with modern open-source technologies to meet your specific business requirements.

responsiveDesign.title: Responsive Web Design
responsiveDesign.description: Beautiful, user-friendly designs that work flawlessly across all devices and screen sizes.

cmsDev.title: CMS Development
cmsDev.description: Modern content management systems that give you full control over your website content with developer-friendly APIs.

maintenance.title: Website Maintenance
maintenance.description: Ongoing support and maintenance to keep your website secure, up-to-date, and performing at its best.

whyOpenSource.title: Why These Values Matter
whyOpenSource.subtitle: In an era of increasing digital surveillance and platform monopolies, these principles are not just nice-to-haves — they are essential for digital freedom and independence.

ownCode.title: You Own Your Code
ownCode.description: Full ownership of your website's source code means no vendor lock-in, no licensing restrictions, and complete freedom to modify, extend, or migrate your site at any time.

ownData.title: You Own Your Data
ownData.description: Your data belongs to you, not the tech giants. We develop solutions that keep your information under your control, with options for self-hosting and data portability.

privacyByDesign.title: Privacy by Design
privacyByDesign.description: We prioritize privacy from the ground up, implementing minimal tracking, secure authentication, and transparent data handling practices that respect your users' privacy.

decentralized.title: Decentralized Architecture
decentralized.description: Break free from centralized platforms that control your online presence. We develop solutions that can be hosted anywhere, giving you independence from Big Tech.

openSourceBase.title: Open-Source Foundation
openSourceBase.description: Built on open-source technologies that are transparent, auditable, and backed by global communities. No hidden code, no proprietary lock-ins.

futureProof.title: Future-Proof & Sustainable
futureProof.description: Open-source technologies evolve with the community and don't disappear when a company fails. Your investment is protected for the long term.

technologies.title: Technologies We Use
technologies.subtitle: We work with proven open-source technologies that deliver reliability, performance, and long-term sustainability.

nextjs.description: Modern React framework for building fast, scalable web applications with excellent developer experience and built-in optimization.

tailwind.description: Utility-first CSS framework for rapidly building modern, responsive UIs with consistent design.

supabase.description: Open-source Firebase alternative providing instant APIs, real-time subscriptions, authentication, and edge functions.

strapi.description: Leading open-source headless CMS giving developers the freedom to choose their preferred tools and frameworks.

payload.description: TypeScript-first headless CMS and application framework built with Node.js, React, and MongoDB.

tina.description: Git-based headless CMS that enables content editing directly on your website with real-time visual editing.

wordpress.description: Mature content management system still popular for certain use cases. We can work with existing WordPress sites.

joomla.description: Flexible content management system with robust user management and multilingual features. Ideal for complex websites.

woocommerce.description: Widely used open-source e-commerce platform built on WordPress. Flexible extensions and large community.

shopware.description: Modern API-first e-commerce platform with powerful customization capabilities. Open-source core with commercial extensions.

hosting.name: Deployment & Hosting
hosting.description: Flexible hosting options from open-source solutions on Linux servers to modern platforms like Vercel and Netlify.

benefits.title: Why Choose Our Web Development Services?
benefits.subtitle: We combine technical expertise with sustainable practices to deliver websites that perform excellently while supporting your values.

automationFirst.title: Automation-First Design
automationFirst.description: We prioritize automated workflows that minimize manual administrative effort while upholding all our core principles. Maximum efficiency without compromise.

openSourceBase.title: Open-Source Foundation
openSourceBase.description: Built on transparent, auditable technologies that give you complete control and freedom. No vendor lock-in, no proprietary black boxes.

seamlessUX.title: Seamless User Experience
seamlessUX.description: Intuitive interfaces that demand minimal effort from users while delivering maximum functionality. Every interaction is designed for simplicity and effectiveness.

devFriendly.title: Developer-Friendly Architecture
devFriendly.description: Clean, maintainable code with excellent documentation and tooling. Built for long-term sustainability and easy maintenance.

process.subtitle: We follow a collaborative, transparent process that ensures your project meets your needs and exceeds your expectations.

meta.title: About us
meta.description: Learn about our mission to extend the lifespan of IT devices and promote sustainable computing.

mission.title: Sustainability meets technology
mission.quote: 10 years is the minimum for a bicycle – and for a laptop too!
mission.paragraph1: As a non-profit association, we have been changing the way technology is used since 2003. Our mission is clear: extend the lifespan of IT devices and reduce e-waste through repair, refurbishment and sustainable practices.
mission.paragraph2: In our premises – a former bank – we have created a meeting place where technology and sustainability come together. Our approach combines hardware recycling with open source software to create sustainable IT solutions for people and the environment.

hero.title: Giving technology a second life
hero.subtitle: For over 20 years we have been fighting against the premature decommissioning of computers and promoting sustainable IT practices.

impactAreas.title: Our impact areas
impactAreas.subtitle: Three pillars, one goal: making technology more sustainable and accessible.

hardware.title: Hardware recycling
hardware.description: We repair and refurbish IT devices of any age and give them a second life. From old MacBooks to vintage computers – every device deserves a second chance.

openSource.title: Open source software
openSource.description: We rely on Linux and other open source solutions. These technologies keep older devices running efficiently and provide security through control over one's own system.

community.title: Community & social
community.description: We create meaningful jobs for people who struggle in the regular job market. With our exchange system, services can be traded for technology.

cta.title: Join our mission
cta.description: Whether you want to have a device repaired, learn more about sustainable IT or support our cause – you are welcome here. Together we make technology more sustainable and accessible for everyone.

meta.title: Become a member
meta.description: Become a member. Voting rights on association decisions, part of the official membership.
meta.ogDescription: Become a member — voting rights, shared responsibility.

benefits.votingDescription: You'll be automatically invited to votes and help shape the direction of the association.
benefits.officialDescription: You're legally part of the association's membership structure under Swiss association law (CC Art. 60 ff).
benefits.financialDescription: Your annual membership fee helps us finance the association sustainably — independently of external funding.

hero.title: Become a member
hero.description: Support the association — legally, financially, and democratically. Fill in the form, transfer your membership fee, done.

items.question: What's the difference between a user account and a membership?
items.answer: A user account is all you need for the marketplace, IT help, and workshops — it's free. An association membership gives you voting rights and makes you officially part of the membership structure. Most users never become members — that's completely fine.

items.question: What do I actually get as a member?
items.answer: Voting rights on association decisions and a place on the official membership list. We don't promise exclusive perks — our services are open to everyone. You become a member because you want to support the association.

items.question: How does joining work?
items.answer: Fill in the form, transfer the annual fee — done. You're a member immediately. No waiting, no approval process.

items.question: How do I pay the annual membership fee?
items.answer: After joining, you'll see the bank details directly on the confirmation page. Bank transfer or TWINT.

items.question: Can I leave at any time?
items.answer: Yes. A simple informal email to the committee is all it takes. Annual fees already paid are not refunded.

meta.title: FAQ – Frequently Asked Questions
meta.description: Answers to common questions about Linux & Open Source, repair, sustainability and e-waste.

hero.title: Frequently Asked Questions
hero.subtitle: Our answers on Linux & Open Source, repair, sustainability and responsible use of resources – in the spirit of our mission against e-waste.

faq.notListed: Can't find your question? We're happy to help in person.

items.q: Do you only work with open-source software?
items.a: No. Open source is our preferred route – for its transparency, security and longevity. Where it makes sense, we also use or integrate proprietary software, always with a focus on usefulness and sustainability.

items.q: Why is open source important to you?
items.a: Open source strengthens self-determination and repairability. It reduces dependencies and makes it possible to use devices for longer – key levers against e-waste.

items.q: What does 'Right to Repair' mean to you?
items.a: We advocate for repairable hardware, documented processes and available spare parts. This extends the lifespan of devices and preserves resources.

items.q: Do you only work with Linux, or also Windows and macOS?
items.a: We work exclusively with Linux and open-source software. We don't install proprietary operating systems (Windows/macOS). Very rarely we make exceptions when a goal demonstrably can't be achieved with open-source tools.

items.q: Do you recommend Linux for everyone?
items.a: Linux is our standard because it's resource-friendly, secure and sustainable. If proprietary tools are absolutely required, we evaluate alternatives – and discuss rare exceptions openly.

items.q: Do you support migration to Linux or open-source alternatives?
items.a: Yes. From pilot projects to full roll-out, we guide individuals, nonprofits and businesses – including training and ongoing support.

items.q: Do you repair devices that weren't bought from you?
items.a: Yes. We repair laptops, PCs and peripherals from a wide range of manufacturers – regardless of where they were purchased.

items.q: Do you repair Apple devices?
items.a: Hardware, yes – provided spare parts are available and a repair makes sense. OS installations are Linux-only; we don't install macOS or Windows.

items.q: How long do repairs take, and what do they cost?
items.a: That depends on the fault and the parts needed. We'll provide a quote on request and keep you fully informed of your options.

items.q: Do you offer data recovery and secure data erasure?
items.a: Yes. We recover data to the best of our ability and securely erase storage media on request using recognised standards (e.g. open-source tools).

items.q: Can I just drop in without an appointment?
items.a: Absolutely. For complex cases or business appointments, a quick message in advance is helpful.

items.q: How do you concretely reduce e-waste?
items.a: Through repairs, component reuse, refurbishing and responsible recycling. We also advise on long-lasting setups and resource-efficient software.

items.q: Do you accept old devices?
items.a: Yes, provided reuse or responsible recycling is possible. We inspect devices on the spot and decide transparently whether they'll be reused or dismantled.

items.q: Do you use second-hand spare parts?
items.a: Where technically sensible, we use tested second-life parts. That saves resources and can cut costs – with no compromise on function.

items.q: Why refurbished instead of new?
items.a: Refurbished devices extend useful life, save CO₂ and raw materials. With the right upgrades, they're often more than good enough – at fair prices.

items.q: Do you stock refurbished devices?
items.a: Yes. We offer carefully checked and refurbished devices with warranty – transparently documented.

items.q: What is the REVAMPED certification?
items.a: REVAMPED marks sustainable, repairable builds with documented provenance, clear upgrade paths and quality assurance.

items.q: Is there a warranty on your devices?
items.a: Yes. Refurbished devices come with a warranty. We state the details transparently for each product.

items.q: How do you handle my data during repairs?
items.a: Confidentially and minimally. We only access data when needed for diagnosis, and protect storage media technically and organisationally.

items.q: Do you offer certified data erasure?
items.a: We offer secure erasure procedures with a record. Just let us know what level of security you need.

items.q: Do you offer workshops or training?
items.a: Yes. Topics include: Linux introduction, repair basics, sustainable IT, open-source tools – for beginners through to advanced users.

items.q: How can I get involved or support you?
items.a: As a volunteer, partner or donor. Sharing your knowledge is also very welcome – together we create real impact.

meta.title: Donate

donate.heroDescription: Every donation saves devices from e-waste, extends lifecycles, and enables technology access for people who couldn't otherwise afford it.
donate.donateDevicesDescription: Hardware you no longer need can give someone else a real opportunity. We accept working or repairable devices, refurbish them, and pass them on.
donate.weAcceptList: Laptops, desktop PCs, monitors, keyboards, mice, printers — working or repairable.
donate.weDoNotAcceptList: Physically damaged devices, broken screens without frames, CRT monitors.
donate.preparationText: Please erase your personal data before dropping off (we can help if needed).
donate.newsletterTitle: We'll keep you informed of how your donation makes an impact.
donate.newsletterDescription: No adverts. Only real updates on devices saved, CO₂ reduced, and people supported.

impact.tier1Description: 1 laptop rescued from e-waste + 285 kg CO₂ avoided.
impact.tier2Description: 2 devices refurbished + a place in our Linux workshop for someone who couldn't otherwise afford it.
impact.tier3Description: 10 devices for educational institutions, social projects, or low-income households.

meta.title: Terms and Conditions
meta.description: Terms and Conditions of revamp-it

section1.title: 1. Scope
section1.body: These Terms and Conditions (T&Cs) apply to all services and offers of {legalName}, {address}.

section2.title: 2. Services
section2.intro: {orgName} is a non-profit association committed to the sustainable refurbishment and reuse of IT equipment. Our services include:
items: Refurbishment and sale of second-hand IT equipment

section3.title: 3. Prices and Payment
section3.body: All prices are in Swiss Francs (CHF) including VAT, unless stated otherwise. Payment terms are specified for each offer.

section4.title: 4. Conclusion of Contract
section4.body: A contract is concluded by placing an order via the platform, in the shop or by e-mail, followed by confirmation from {orgName}. Representations on the website constitute a non-binding invitation to submit an offer.

section5.title: 5. Right of Withdrawal
section5.body: For in-store purchases there is no statutory right of withdrawal, as Swiss law does not provide for this in physical shops. For online purchases, we grant a voluntary right of return of 14 days from receipt, provided the goods are returned unused and in their original packaging.

section6.title: 6. Warranty
section6.body: {orgName} provides a warranty on refurbished devices in accordance with the applicable legal provisions. Exact warranty terms are communicated at the point of purchase. For devices sold via the P2P marketplace by third parties, {orgName} assumes no warranty.

section7.title: 7. Liability
section7.body: {orgName} is liable within the scope of the applicable legal provisions. Liability for minor negligence is excluded to the extent permitted by law.

section8.title: 8. Data Protection
section8.body: Personal data is processed in accordance with our privacy policy. Registered users can export their data at any time.

section9.title: 9. Final Provisions
section9.body: Should individual provisions of these T&Cs prove invalid, the validity of the remaining provisions is not affected. Swiss law applies. The place of jurisdiction is Zurich, Switzerland.

section10.title: 10. Changes
section10.body: {orgName} reserves the right to change these T&Cs at any time. The current version is available on this page. Legal information about the provider can be found in the legal notice.

meta.title: Privacy Policy
meta.description: Privacy Policy of revamp-it

section2.title: 2. Collection and Processing of Personal Data
section2.intro: We collect personal data when you register on our platform, use our services or get in touch with us. This includes:

section3.title: 3. Purpose of Data Processing
items: Providing and managing your user account
Communication regarding our offers (with consent only)

section4.title: 4. Data Disclosure
section4.body: Your data is not sold to third parties. Disclosure only occurs where necessary to provide our services (e.g. to repair providers for booked repairs) or where we are legally required to do so.

section5.title: 5. Data Security
section5.body: We implement technical and organisational measures to protect your data against unauthorised access, loss or misuse. Passwords are stored exclusively in hashed form.

section6.title: 6. Cookies
section6.body: Our platform uses functional cookies for session management and authentication. Analytical cookies are only set with your consent.

section7.title: 7. Your Rights
section7.exportText: Registered users can download a full copy of their data at any time under
section7.contactText: For all other enquiries, please contact:

section8.title: 8. Retention Period
section8.body: We store your personal data only as long as necessary for the stated purposes or as required by statutory retention obligations. Account and profile data is removed after account deletion, unless legal obligations (e.g. fiscal retention of invoices) prevent this.

section9.title: 9. Legal Basis
section9.body: Processing is based on the Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection (FADP) and, where applicable, on Art. 6(1) GDPR (contract performance, legitimate interests, consent).

section10.title: 10. Applicable Law
section10.body: This privacy policy is subject to the Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection (FADP) and, where applicable, the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

meta.title: Legal Notice
meta.description: Legal notice and legal information

impressum.title: Legal Notice
impressum.legalFormValue: Association under Art. 60 et seq. ZGB (Swiss Civil Code)
impressum.purposeText: {description} {orgName} is a non-profit association committed to the free exchange of technology, the promotion of open-source hardware and software, and the sustainable refurbishment and reuse of IT equipment.
impressum.disclaimerP1: The author accepts no liability whatsoever regarding the accuracy, correctness, currency, reliability and completeness of the information on this website.
impressum.disclaimerP2: Liability claims against the author for material or immaterial damage arising from access to or use or non-use of published information, from misuse of the connection or from technical malfunctions are excluded.
impressum.disclaimerP3: All offers are non-binding. The author expressly reserves the right to change, supplement or delete parts of the pages or the entire offering without prior notice, or to cease publication temporarily or permanently.
impressum.linksDisclaimerText: References and links to third-party websites are outside our area of responsibility. All liability for such websites is disclaimed. Access to and use of such websites is at the sole risk of the user concerned.
impressum.copyrightText: The copyright and all other rights to content, images, photos or other files on this website belong exclusively to {legalName} or the specifically named rights holders. The prior written consent of the copyright holders must be obtained for the reproduction of any elements.
impressum.furtherLegalText: See also our privacy policy and our terms and conditions.

hero.subtitle: What we do and how we do it

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.text: {description} As a {legalForm} (founded in {foundingYear}), we are committed to giving second-hand hardware a second life, making open software available to everyone, and helping people learn to use technology on their own terms.

howWeWork.title: How We Work
howWeWork.subtitle: Our three community platforms connect people with technology.

platforms.title: Marketplace
platforms.description: Community platform for second-hand IT equipment. Users can create listings, buy and sell – free of charge.

platforms.title: IT Help
platforms.description: Connecting volunteer technicians for computer repairs. Human-to-human help, local and straightforward.

platforms.title: Workshops
platforms.description: Courses on Linux, hardware, programming and AI. Sharing knowledge and learning from each other – in person in Zurich.

finances.title: Finances
finances.text: As a non-profit association, we make our finances public. The annual report is presented at the General Meeting and is available to members.

meta.title: REVAMPED Certification | Sustainable Computer Builds
meta.description: Learn about our exclusive REVAMPED certification – guaranteeing sustainability, quality and performance in every computer we build.

hero.title: The REVAMPED Certification
hero.subtitle: Every computer we build receives our exclusive REVAMPED label – a guarantee of sustainability, quality and performance that sets a new standard in the industry.

inAction.title: Real REVAMPED Computers in Action
inAction.subtitle: Sustainable technology that people love to use. Our REVAMPED certification isn't just a promise – it's a visible commitment to quality and environmental responsibility.

meaning.title: What "REVAMPED" Means
meaning.subtitle: Our certification is more than a label – it's a comprehensive standard ensuring every computer meets our strict criteria for sustainability, quality and performance.

pillars.title: Sustainability First
pillars.description: We prioritise used and refurbished components wherever possible, drastically reducing environmental impact while maintaining peak performance. We apply circular economy principles to every build.

pillars.title: Quality Guaranteed
pillars.description: Every component is thoroughly tested, cleaned and verified to meet our strict performance and reliability standards. Quality is never compromised for sustainability.

pillars.title: AI-Optimised
pillars.description: Our AI ensures perfect component compatibility and optimal performance tuning for your specific use case. Every build is intelligently designed around your needs.

certificate.title: Certificate of Authenticity
certificate.subtitle: Every REVAMPED computer comes with a detailed certificate providing full transparency about your sustainable build.
items: CO₂ emissions saved versus new build
certificate.digitalDesc: Your certificate includes both a physical sticker and a digital QR code for instant verification and detailed component information.

stickers.title: The REVAMPED Label Collection
stickers.subtitle: Each design represents our commitment to sustainable computing and quality assurance.

original.title: Original Design
original.description: Clean, professional certification mark emphasising our laptop-focused sustainability approach.

eco.title: Eco Variant
eco.description: Extended design highlighting our environmental focus and circular economy principles.

digital.title: Digital Certificate
digital.description: QR-code-linked verification system with blockchain-backed authenticity records.

cta.title: Join the REVAMPED Revolution
cta.subtitle: When you choose a REVAMPED computer, you're not just getting a great machine – you're making a statement about the future of technology. Sustainable, powerful, and built with purpose.

meta.title: Our Locations
meta.description: From the Toni Dairy to today – the story of our spaces and our vision for a new community hub in Zurich.

hero.title: From Dairy to Community Space
hero.subtitle: Since 2003, we've been moving around Zurich. Now we're searching for a new home – a place where sustainability, technology and community come together.

history.title: Our Journey Through Zurich
history.subtitle: Over 20 years, multiple locations, one mission: using technology sustainably.

locations.name: Toni Dairy
locations.description: Our birthplace in the old cheese cellar. This is where the legendary Debian 10th anniversary party happened on the roof.

locations.name: Reformed Church Wipkingen
locations.description: On the 6th/7th floor of the church tower and in the cellars. This is where our first social projects started.

locations.name: Röschibachstrasse
locations.description: A large space with many workstations. Expansion of our workshop.

future.title: Our Dream Location
future.subtitle: We need to leave our current premises by the end of 2026. This is our chance to create something bigger.
future.visionDescription: An open space where technology, sustainability and community come together. Shop, workshop, coworking, events – all under one roof.
future.conceptCaption: Concept visualisation of our ideal community space

features.name: Shop Area
features.description: Exhibition space for refurbished devices with expert advice

features.name: Repair Workshop
features.description: Workstations for technicians and community repairs

features.name: Event Space
features.description: Room for workshops, meetups and small events (15–20 people)

features.name: Mini Library
features.description: A few shelves of tech books, magazines and resources

features.name: Coffee Corner
features.description: Meeting point for informal exchange and networking

features.name: Kitchen
features.description: Fully equipped kitchen for zero food waste initiatives

params.title: What We're Looking For
params.subtitle: The parameters for our ideal location
params.idealText: Ground floor or easily accessible, wheelchair-accessible, loading bay or delivery access, in a vibrant neighbourhood with good public transport links.
params.tipText: Know a suitable space? We'd love to hear from you!

impact.title: Why a Bigger Space Means More Impact
impact.subtitle: With more space, we can multiply our KPIs – save more devices, train more people, achieve more impact.

items.reason: Dedicated event space for regular courses
More internship places with larger premises

donateCta.title: Support Our Location Fund
donateCta.description: With a bigger budget, we can find a better location – and multiply our impact on the environment and society. Every contribution helps us realise our dream of a community space.

shopLinks.title: All Shopping Options
shopLinks.subtitle: In-person and online – choose what suits you best.

hero.subtitle: Help us create high-quality, ad-free content about sustainable technology, open source, and the future of computing.

noAds.title: No Ads
noAds.description: We will never run advertising or sell your data.

communityDriven.title: Community-Driven
communityDriven.description: By the community, for the community – with no commercial interests.

promise.title: Our Promise
promise.intro: RevampIT is not a company – it's a movement. We believe that high-quality information about sustainable technology should be accessible to everyone, without paywalls or intrusive advertising.

oneTime.title: One-Time Donation
oneTime.description: Buy us a coffee or support us with any amount you like.

monthly.title: Monthly Support
monthly.description: Become a supporter and help us grow sustainably.

support.thanks: Thank you so much for supporting our mission for sustainable, high-quality tech content.
